title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class AI model with capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The model includes built-in safety systems that restrict its use in high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry to prevent misuse.
writeup: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a public-facing version of its Mythos-class frontier model, with a classifier layer that routes flagged queries in cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, and model distillation to Claude Opus 4.8 rather than the full model. The safeguard triggers in fewer than 5% of sessions, leaving the rest on Fable 5 proper, which sets state-of-the-art marks across software engineering, knowledge work, vision, and scientific reasoning. A parallel release, Claude Mythos 5, carries the same weights with guards lifted and is restricted to vetted organizations via a US government collaboration called Project Glasswing. The release also mandates 30-day traffic retention for all Fable 5 and Mythos 5 customers, including enterprises that previously held zero-retention agreements.
key_points: Fable 5 is the publicly available Mythos-class model with a separate classifier system that intercepts high-risk requests and falls back to Opus 4.8; Anthropic reports this fallback fires in fewer than 5% of sessions based on early data.

Stripe reported that Fable 5 completed a codebase-wide migration of a 50-million-line Ruby codebase in a single day, a task Stripe estimated would have taken a full engineering team over two months manually.

Hex, an analytics company, found Fable 5 to be the first model to reach 90% on their core analytics benchmark of complex long-running analytical tasks, with the model showing strong judgment on the hardest questions.

Mythos-class models outperformed specialized protein language models on a challenging adeno-associated virus design task without explicit training for that domain, a capability Anthropic considers dual-use for both gene therapy and potential bioweapon design.

An external bug bounty running over 1,000 hours produced no universal jailbreaks, though the UK AI Safety Institute made partial progress toward one within an initial testing window; Anthropic frames its goal as making remaining jailbreaks slow and costly to execute at scale rather than impossible.

Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens, double the price of Opus 4.8 but less than half the price of the earlier Mythos preview; Fable 5 is included in Pro, Max, Team, and enterprise subscription plans through June 22 before shifting to usage-credit billing.
